sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Sevilla have had a mixed run in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins but suffering 3 losses, including heavy defeats to Atlético Madrid (3-0) and Mallorca (1-3). Osasuna also show inconsistency with 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses in their recent 5 games, struggling to find the net consistently. Both teams are looking to bounce back in this encounter.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last 2 head-to-head clashes, Osasuna have had the upper hand, with a 1-0 victory in April 2025 and a 1-1 draw in December 2024 at Sevilla’s home. Osasuna remain unbeaten in their last 8 matches against Sevilla, which could provide a psychological edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Sevilla, sitting 13th with 13 points, have scored 17 goals this season (6th in La Liga) but struggle defensively, conceding 19 (16th). Osasuna, 15th with 11 points, have managed only 9 goals while conceding 12, indicating a more conservative but less effective attack. Sevilla’s higher possession (52.9%) and tackling stats (19 per game) could dominate play.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Sevilla’s Ruben Vargas (4 assists, 6.87 rating) and in-form defender Kike Salas (8.3 average rating) will be crucial, though they miss forward Isaac Romero. Osasuna lack key striker Ante Budimir (4 goals) and midfielder Lucas Torró, relying on Jon Moncayola (7.16 rating) for creativity in midfield.

sections.subtitle: Match Expectations
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (39% home win, 34% draw, 27% away win) and Sevilla’s home advantage at Ramón Sánchez Pizjuán, they are slight favorites despite recent form. Osasuna’s defensive setup and historical edge in this fixture suggest a tight, low-scoring game. Expect a cautious battle with potential for a draw.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline remains level with neither Sevilla nor Osasuna managing to break the deadlock. Both teams have shown intent in the first half, with Sevilla holding a slight edge in possession and showing more consistent presence in the final third. However, Osasuna have matched them in terms of entries into dangerous areas and have been competitive in physical duels, winning a higher percentage of aerial challenges. The game has been tightly contested, with disciplinary issues creeping in for Sevilla as they've picked up more yellow cards compared to their opponents.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: Looking at the flow of the game, Sevilla started strongly with notable attacking spells early on, pushing forward with intent in the opening minutes. Osasuna responded with their own periods of pressure, particularly in the middle of the half, where they managed to shift momentum in their favor with sustained attacks. Towards the latter stages of the half, Sevilla regained control, mounting several dangerous moves and finishing the half on the front foot. Osasuna, however, showed resilience with brief moments of threat before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Sevilla's dominance in possession is clear, allowing them to dictate the tempo for significant portions of the half. They've also made more entries into the penalty area and won possessions in the final third, suggesting they’ve been closer to creating clear-cut chances. Osasuna, on the other hand, have been slightly more effective in aerial battles and have matched Sevilla in terms of final third entries, indicating they are not far behind in terms of attacking threat. Both teams have similar expected goals figures, reflecting a balanced first half in terms of quality of opportunities created.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Player performances
halftimeSections.text: For Sevilla, Peque Fernández has stood out with a strong individual display, contributing creatively and posing a threat in the opposition half. Gabriel Suazo has also been influential with key contributions in build-up play. On Osasuna's side, Abel Bretones and Alejandro Catena have been solid, with the latter showing potential threat from set-pieces. Raúl García de Haro has been a focal point in attack for the visitors, looking to capitalize on any chances that come his way.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Sevilla have leaned on their ability to control the game through possession, often looking to build attacks methodically. However, their discipline has been a concern with multiple bookings, which could impact their approach in the second half if they risk further cards. Osasuna have adopted a more direct style at times, focusing on physicality and quick transitions to exploit spaces. Their ability to win aerial duels could be a key factor if they target set-pieces or long balls into the box.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Sevilla push harder for the opener, given their stronger possession stats and home advantage at the Ramón Sánchez Pizjuán. They may look to capitalize on their final third presence and convert it into goals, possibly through players like Peque Fernández. Osasuna, however, will not sit back and are expected to remain a threat on the counter, especially through Raúl García de Haro. With both teams showing attacking intent, the game could open up, and a goal seems likely if either side sharpens their finishing.
